name: Vitamin D (Calciferols)
contraindications: Avoid chronic high-dose unsupervised use due to hypercalcemia risk

sources.title: Vitamin D Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)
Institute of Medicine vitamin D report (2011)

description: Vitamin D2/D3 from skin, diet, and supplements are converted to active metabolites.
summary: Essential prohormone nutrient for calcium/bone health; both deficiency and chronic excess are clinically important.
mechanism: Increases calcium/phosphorus absorption and supports bone mineralization via vitamin D receptor signaling.
safetyNotes: Chronic excess can cause hypercalcemia and renal complications.
interactions: Thiazides may increase hypercalcemia risk; glucocorticoids can reduce vitamin D effects.
dosage: RDA framework about 600–800 IU/day; upper-limit context about 4,000 IU/day without supervision.
preparation: Available as D3 and D2, including combined calcium+vitamin D products.
regulatoryStatus: Essential nutrient; OTC supplements and high-dose prescription regimens exist.
